documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone git@github.com:andresriancho/enumerate-iam.git; pip install -r requirements.txt. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `./enumerate-iam.py --access-key AKIA... --secret-key StF0q...`. Further invocations from upstream: `2019-05-10 15:57:58,447 - 21345 - [INFO] Starting permission enumeration for access-key-id "AKIA..."`; `2019-05-10 15:58:01,537 - 21345 - [INFO] -- {`. name: enumerate-iam

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need recon, use enumerate-iam because Found a set of AWS credentials and have no idea which permissions it might have?.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./enumerate-iam.py --access-key AKIA... --secret-key StF0q...` as documented upstream for enumerate-iam.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `2019-05-10 15:57:58,447 - 21345 - [INFO] Starting permission enumeration for access-key-id "AKIA..."` as documented upstream for enumerate-iam.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `2019-05-10 15:58:01,537 - 21345 - [INFO] -- {` as documented upstream for enumerate-iam.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `2019-05-10 15:58:26,709 - 21345 - [INFO] -- gamelift.list_builds() worked!` as documented upstream for enumerate-iam.
When chaining recon tools, pair enumerate-iam with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `enumerate-iam --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
